source_jobs.title: Washington, DC Remote Mental Health Therapist
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT MARVIN BEHAVIORAL HEALTH

At Marvin Behavioral Health, we deliver teletherapy designed specifically for doctors, nurses, and other healthcare professionals, addressing the unique challenges of their demanding work. Marvin was developed by Healthcare Leader and CEO John Bracaglia, along with a psychiatrist co-founder, both sharing a vision to build a company that gives back to the medical community.

We are a multidisciplinary team of therapists, psychiatrists, engineers, data scientists, and healthcare leaders united by one mission: reshaping mental health support for the healthcare workforce. Join our clinical team to make a lasting impact by providing therapy to those who care for others!




WHY JOIN?

As a clinician-founded company, we understand what providers need to thrive: autonomy, community, and support.

 - 100% remote work – freedom to live and work anywhere in the U.S.

 - Control your schedule – choose your hours and caseload (starting at 10-15 hours per week)

 - No-show & late-cancellation compensation – your time is valued

 - Full administrative support – we fill your caseload, manage scheduling and billing, and offer real-time support from our Provider Success team

 - Client matching & crisis support – We’ll match you with clients who fit your expertise, and they’ll have 24/7 access to Marvin’s crisis support

 - Professional perks – free CEUs, compensated onboarding, and malpractice insurance

 - Community & connection – join peer consultation groups and a supportive network of fellow clinicians

 - Mission-driven work – make an impact by supporting the mental health of healthcare professionals




YOUR ROLE

As a therapist with Marvin, you’ll provide evidence-based virtual therapy to adult healthcare workers. Role details include:

 - Deliver high-quality, confidential teletherapy sessions on our HIPAA-compliant video platform (Zoom) to doctors, nurses and healthcare professionals

 - Use evidence-based practices such as Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Dialectical Behavioral Therapy (DBT), Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT), Internal Family Systems (IFS), Eye Movement Desensitization and Reprocessing (EMDR), or Gottman Method

 - Complete biopsychosocial assessments, diagnoses, treatment plans, and progress notes in our streamlined EHR

 - Incorporate measurement-based tools (e.g., PHQ-9, GAD-7) to monitor progress and improve outcomes




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - Independent licensure in Washington, DC as an LMFT, LICSW, LPC, or Licensed Psychologist (See our Careers Page https://www.meetmarvin.com/for-therapists for other states we’re hiring in)

 - Minimum of 2 years of post-licensure experience providing therapy to adults

 - Experience providing therapy to healthcare professionals, mental healthcare workers, and/or first responders

 - Availability for at least 10-15 hours per week

 - Comfort delivering therapy via video platforms, using EHRs, digital scheduling tools, and Google Workspace

 - Ability to incorporate assessment and measurement-based tools (i.e. PHQ-9) to monitor progress and improve treatment outcomes

 - Experience utilizing evidence-based practices such as CBT, DBT, ACT, IFS, EMDR or Gottman Method

 - Commitment to practicing cultural humility and patient-centered care

 - Passion for supporting the mental health of healthcare workers!



COMPENSATION

We value your time and expertise. As a remote independent contractor, you’ll receive competitive hourly pay. Because your time matters, you’ll be compensated 50% of your rate for late cancellations or no-shows.

 - LMFT, LICSW, LPC: $60 - $70/hr (depending on experience)

 - Licensed Psychologist: $70 - $80/hr




NEXT STEP

 - Apply today to schedule an exploratory video call with one of our Talent Acquisition partners and learn more about joining Marvin!




INCLUSION & ACCESSIBILITY

Marvin Health is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer, and we celebrate diversity in all its forms. We are committed to creati

source_jobs.title: Client Success Manager
source_jobs.description:
CLIENT SUCCESS MANAGER


About Marvin AI



Marvin is on a mission to end healthcare’s workforce crisis. Marvin partners with many of the country’s top-ranked hospitals including Cedars-Sinai in Los Angeles, the VA, Jefferson Health and Geisinger in Pennsylvania, and the Harvard Department of Psychiatry, to provide a proprietary retention and resiliency platform for their physicians, nurses, and staff. Services include confidential teletherapy with specialized therapists, an AI coaching model built for clinicians, medication management/psychiatry, and well-being workshops. All programming is geared towards helping Marvin health system clients to improve clinician retention, patient outcomes, and team burnout, driving their overall effectiveness and resilience.

Role Overview

We’re looking for a high-impact east coast based Client Success Manager to own and grow relationships with our health system partners. This role is responsible for driving adoption, retention, and expansion across a portfolio of enterprise accounts.

The ideal candidate brings experience managing complex healthcare clients in a high-growth environment and is comfortable operating at both the executive and operational levels.

Join us in building something that meaningfully improves the lives of healthcare workers.


KEY FOCUS AREAS INCLUDE:

 - Own a portfolio of health system accounts, driving retention, satisfaction, and growth

 - Build and maintain strong relationships with C-suite and senior stakeholders across client organizations

 - Lead strategic account planning and quarterly business reviews (QBRs) tied to measurable outcomes

 - Drive product adoption and engagement across client organizations, ensuring long-term value realization

 - Identify and execute opportunities for account expansion (additional sites, services, or programs)

 - Partner cross-functionally (product, clinical, ops) to advocate for client needs and influence roadmap priorities

 - Track and analyze key success metrics, including activation, engagement, clinical outcomes, and satisfaction

 - Proactively identify risks and develop mitigation strategies to ensure client success

 - Partners with CS leadership to define, refine, and continuously improve customer success best practices, processes, and playbooks as the organization grows and scales

 


QUALIFICATIONS

 - 5–8+ years of experience in client success, account management, or related roles

 - Experience owning a book of business of $1M+ in ARR

 - Experience managing enterprise or strategic accounts, ideally within healthcare or health tech

 - Experience working with employer-sponsored benefits or benefits platforms (nice to have)

 - Proven ability to engage and influence C-suite stakeholders

 - Strong track record of driving retention, adoption, and/or revenue expansion

 - Comfortable navigating complex organizations and ambiguous environments

 - Excellent communication, presentation, and stakeholder management skills

 - Highly organized with strong project management capabilities

 - Experience with CRM and analytics tools (e.g., Salesforce, Excel, or similar)

 

Why Marvin AI?



 - We’re truly mission-driven. We founded Marvin on the belief that healthcare workers deserve the care and support they provide to their patients, and that existing solutions to growing healthcare burnout are ineffective.
   



Preferred candidates based in the NYC metro region, or midatlantic. East coast time zone required. Remote role with occasional onsite client visits and in-office team building in our NYC office.

We are a multidisciplinary team of therapists, psychiatrists, engineers, data scientists, and healthcare leaders united by one mission: reshaping mental health support for the healthcare workforce. Join our clinical team to make a lasting impact by providing therapy to those who care for others!




WHY JOIN?

As a clinician-founded company, we understand what providers need to thrive: autonomy, community, and support.

 - No-show & late-cancellation compensation – your time is valued

 - Experience utilizing evidence-based practices such as CBT, DBT, ACT, IFS, EMDR or Gottman Method

 - Commitment to practicing cultural humility and patient-centered care

 - Passion for supporting the mental health of healthcare workers!



COMPENSATION

We value your time and expertise. As a remote independent contractor, you’ll receive competitive hourly pay. Because your time matters, you’ll be compensated 50% of your rate for late cancellations or no-shows.

 - LMFT, LCSW, LPC: $60 - $70/hr (depending on experience)

 - Licensed Psychologist: $70 - $80/hr




NEXT STEP

 - Apply today to schedule an exploratory video call with one of our Talent Acquisition partners and learn more about joining Marvin!




INCLUSION

source_jobs.title: Healthcare Partnerships Sales Executive
source_jobs.description:
Healthcare Partnerships Sales Executive- Job Description



Marvin is a company on a mission to end healthcare worker burnout. Marvin partners with many of the country’s top hospitals, including Cedars-Sinai, Jefferson Health and Geisinger in Pennsylvania, and the Harvard Department of Psychiatry, to provide a proprietary mental health care platform for their physicians, nurses, and staff.



We are seeking a Healthcare Partnerships Sales Executive to own the new health system acquisition and pipeline development. Reporting to the VP of Sales and Partnerships, this individual contributor will grow Marvin’s health system pipeline and new logos. The ideal candidate will possess strong interpersonal skills, strategic thinking abilities, attention to detail, and a proven track record of success in sales and business development. They will have experience building and owning a sales pipeline, integrating feedback and executing on the outreach process, as a strategic sales executive in the healthcare vertical calling on C-suite executives at health systems.



This position is available in Denver, Los Angeles, New York, the candidate will be expected to spend 1-2 days per week in the office, with the remaining days remote.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Engage in day-to-day sales operations, prospecting, meeting with health system executives, and developing strategies to exceed quota.

 - Identify and establish partnerships and strategic relationships to expand Marvin’s reach.

 - Lead negotiations and contract redlining with health system clients and partners to secure favorable terms and signed agreements.

 - Develop and implement strategic plans to drive business growth and achieve revenue targets.

 - Conduct market research and analysis to identify business opportunities and market trends.

 - Represent the company at industry events, conferences, and networking opportunities to enhance Marvin’s visibility and reputation.

QUALIFICATIONS

 - Proven experience in a senior sales executive role, with a minimum of 5 years of experience in sales and business development, including at least 2 years selling into health systems.

 - Strong strategic planning and execution skills, with the ability to drive organizational change and achieve measurable results.

 - Demonstrated sales excellence and quota achievement

 - Demonstrated success selling to health system C-suite executives (e.g., CMO, CNO, CWO, CHRO) and working with or selling to clinicians.

 - Experience creating and delivering strategic sales presentations and a comfort operating with ambiguity of an early-stage startup.

 - Significant experience and comfort using AI tools

 - Excellent communication, interpersonal, and negotiation skills, with the ability to build and maintain effective relationships at all levels of the organization.

 - Self-starter with an analytical mindset with the ability to analyze complex issues, make data-driven decisions, and solve problems creatively.

 - Strong ethical and professional integrity, with a commitment to upholding the organization's values and standards.

COMPENSATION

 - The base salary range for this role is $115,000–$135,000. This is a commission-eligible role with an additional target variable component of approximately $165,000 (uncapped), for an on-target total compensation of approximately $290,000. Compensation also includes equity in the company. Actual base pay within the range will depend on the candidate’s experience, skills, and qualifications. We offer a comprehensive benefits package including medical, dental, and vision insurance, retirement savings, and paid time off.

notes: Marvin Behavioral Health is a healthcare-focused company with a multidisciplinary team, but the job postings suggest a non-tech satellite organization with a small engineering presence.
